Transcrição Completa: 36 Is It Better To Have A Contract Or Not

 

[00:00] [music]

 

[00:03] In business and in your agency, you're probably asking yourself, should I run a contract towards every client that I get or should I go no contract?

 

[00:12] One thing that I've done in my agency is I've been very consistent with this from the start. I felt that this has worked best for us and I've tried all the different ways you could do this.

 

[00:23] When we offer contracts, let's say that are 90 days or 6 months long or a year long, there are so many different questions that the client then has to ask themselves, especially when they've never done business with you and you haven't gotten them any results yet.

 

[00:39] They say, "Do I want to be contractually obligated to somebody that I don't know can get me the result that I'm desiring, which is why I'm going in to deal with the deal with this person?"

 

[00:51] "Am I going to be able to pay that amount that they're asking every month?" And if they're not making the money from it yet from what you're offering, they have a hard time justifying that.

 

[01:01] So, it creates additional objections. It creates some friction in the deal, and it pushes out the amount. It puts a lot of time in between how long it's going to take to get the deal done.

 

[01:12] And to be clear, you're in a saturated market nowadays. There's probably for every business that you're pitching, probably two other people that are pitching them every other month or so.

 

[01:23] Depending on what market you're operating in and how positioned that business is, they could be getting two a day depending on who you're pitching. Okay?

 

[01:32] So, my point is, when you're approaching businesses and you say, "I want to put you in a contract." You better have some serious results and some some proven experience that you're going to transition into this person's business to make them more confident in signing that contract with you if you would take that approach.

 

[01:50] I've never taken that approach. I don't like it. It's not a projected bias from me onto the client. I feel that from doing that, there's always that additional resistance and friction that comes in the deals when we've tried contracts.

 

[02:04] What I prefer is a 30-day agreement that is a recurring deal that requires a 30-day notice to get out of.

 

[02:14] So, when we send our contracts, which are just legal agreements to people is what we call them. We don't call them contracts, we call them agreements.

 

[02:23] What'll happen in the verbiage on that agreement? It just simply states, "Hey, this is a 30-day agreement. It's not a contract. And as soon as you want to stop doing business with us, just give us a 30-day heads up."

 

[02:35] That means every time we take a deal, it's not a 30-day deal. It's at least a 60-day deal.

 

[02:42] Because if somebody does a deal with us and they want to quit after 5 days, if they let us know that they want to quit, well, they still have the recurring portion of the deal coming up.

 

[02:52] They still have a full 25 days that we have to do whatever it is we need to do to get them additional results, which we've seen from experience helps a lot.

 

[03:01] So, as an example, let's say you have a client where you have to build them a funnel. You have to build them some marketing automation, like some email follow-up, and you have to advertise for them.

 

[03:12] Maybe the first two weeks of your 30-day relationship, that work is required just to have the potential to get results because, of course, advertising would start after that.

 

[03:23] So, for the first two weeks, let's say the client gets all angsty, right? And they're not really liking the process because they're not getting results yet.

 

[03:31] They're spending money on you as the service provider, but they haven't made an ROI yet, of course, because there's nothing to make an ROI with. You have to build it.

 

[03:41] Well, in that process, let's say the client is like, "You know what? I don't really have that much money right now. I'm kind of getting a little anxiety about the money I have to spend on ads coming up, and I got your renewal coming up in two weeks after this."

 

[03:54] "You know, I I don't want to continue the deal." Well, if they give you a 30-day notice on day 15 of the month, that means that the deal will still end in 30 days from that date.

 

[04:07] So, there's still one renewal period 15 days later. That's where you explain to the client in a confident way, "Client, we do this intentionally for people who like to squirm while we're in the process of getting results."

 

[04:21] "It starts off with us needing to put in the work to enable you to have the positioning to get a result. What we got to build is what gets you the results. Then we got to send traffic to it."

 

[04:32] "So, we got to start running ads. We're probably going to have to pivot our ads. We're probably going to have to tweak a bunch of stuff after we've started sending traffic."

 

[04:41] "We are in this together. We understand the process and we want to make sure you do, too."

 

[04:46] And this is where you of course want to make sure you're explaining the things to give the client more confidence to avoid them saying, "Hey, I want to quit."

 

[04:54] We position our clients to not be able to quit without giving us a 30-day notice. So, you're still going to have to spend in two weeks a renewal with us.

 

[05:04] And on top of that, you're still going to have to spend the ad spend to get the results here in these next couple days.

 

[05:11] The process itself is necessary for us to get to the desired result, which we agreed upon when we went into this agency client relationship together.

 

[05:20] If you want to give us a 30-day notice, that's fine. But in fairness and in all ethics of business, you're still going to do the deal with us for that upcoming renewal and you're still going to have the ad spend that you're going to incur.

 

[05:33] And this, of course, comes down to the expectations that you set when the person went into the deal for how they react from this point forward.

 

[05:41] They're either going to be reminded that they're working with experts who are confident that the results are coming, but are simply in the process of generating those results.

 

[05:51] Or they're immediately going to find out at this point that they went into business with an amateur who's not confident about getting them a result, and they simply are going to have a higher cost to renewal and they're going to have the advertising cost with somebody who's not as confident.

 

[06:06] You want to remember certainty and confidence is what gives the other person the ability to trust you at higher levels. Your integrity is what gets the other person to trust you at higher levels.

 

[06:17] So before you ever do a deal with somebody, just make it very clear, "Hey, you're not in a contract. Should you ever want to get out of this, you just have to give us a 30-day notice" and then throw out a hypothetical situation.

 

[06:29] "So that would mean" and then explain the process. "You know, we have to do this here. That's not going to be money driven. We have to do this here, you know, so for that long, you're not going to be making any money."

 

[06:40] "And then right here, that's when we start advertising. So if you quit anywhere in here, you're still going to have to pay this and you're still going to have to pay that. It's not a contract. That's just how we do business."

 

[06:51] "So, you don't hurt us while we take on the deal with you. We're only taking on the deal with you because we're confident to get you the result."

 

[07:00] Take that approach. That'll help you a lot. And that'll make sure that you don't need to do a contract, which creates the additional friction, and it'll also make sure you have more predictability in the revenue that comes into your business.

 

[07:13] Hope this helps and I'll see you in the next [video cuts off]

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Acordo de 30 dias (não 90 dias, 6 meses ou 1 ano)

Mínimo de 60 dias de relacionamento (30 dias + 30 dias de aviso)

Exemplo: Aviso no dia 15 = mais 30 dias = 45 dias totais

Primeiras 2 semanas: construção (funil, automação, setup)

2 competidores pitchando a cada mês (ou 2 por dia dependendo do nicho)

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

 

Estrutura de Acordo (NÃO Contrato):

Termo: "Agreement" (acordo) em vez de "Contract" (contrato)

Duração: 30 dias recorrentes

Saída: Requer aviso prévio de 30 dias

Resultado: Sempre no mínimo 60 dias de trabalho

 

Processo de Implementação:

Semanas 1-2: Construção do funil e automação de marketing

Semana 2+: Início dos anúncios

Período contínuo: Ajustes e otimizações

 

Gestão de Objeções do Cliente:

Explicar processo completo antes do fechamento

Usar cenários hipotéticos para educar

Demonstrar confiança e certeza nos resultados

Posicionar como proteção mútua, não punição

 

PROBLEMAS COM CONTRATOS LONGOS:

Criam objeções adicionais

Geram fricção no fechamento

Atrasam o processo de venda

Cliente questiona comprometimento sem resultados prévios

Cliente preocupa-se com capacidade de pagamento mensal

 

VANTAGENS DO ACORDO DE 30 DIAS:

Menos resistência do cliente

Fechamento mais rápido

Proteção para a agência (sempre 60+ dias)

Tempo para gerar resultados mesmo se cliente desistir cedo

Previsibilidade de receita

 

ELEMENTOS PSICOLÓGICOS:

Certeza e confiança = Maior confiança do cliente

Integridade = Maior confiança do cliente

Postura de expert vs amador determina reação do cliente

Expectativas claras no início previnem problemas futuros

 

ESTRUTURA DE COMUNICAÇÃO:

Explicar processo completo no início

Detalhar timeline de trabalho

Esclarecer quando começam os gastos com ads

Apresentar cenário hipotético de desistência

Reforçar que é proteção mútua, não contrato punitivo

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Cliente paga setup + pelo menos 1 renovação

Cliente arca com ad spend durante período de aviso

Previsibilidade aumentada de receita mensal

Proteção contra prejuízo da agência em trabalho inicial

 

DURAÇÃO: 07:13 FORMATO: Vídeo educacional direto para câmera com música de fundo
